---
title: "Week 4 — Telling Stories with Data"
author: "Mike Boland"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme:
      - default
      - ../shared/styles/boland-reveal.scss
    css: ../shared/styles/accessibility.css
    slide-number: true
    hash: true
    center: false
    transition: fade
execute:
  echo: false
---

# Communicating Data

*Turn descriptive statistics and visuals into a story someone can act on.*


::: notes
Week 1: what is your data  
Week 2: summarizing  
Week 3: visualizing  
Week 4: interpretation and narrative
:::

## One Set of Principles  

<br>

- Writing  
<br>

- Visuals  
<br>

-  Speaking  
<br>

:::: fragment
::: {.callout-note title="Small Medium Large"}
The same principles will apply across different media.
:::
::::

::: notes
This frames everything that follows.
:::

## Principle 1: One Message

<br>
**Writing**  
Lead with the main conclusion.

<br>
**Visual**  
One message per chart.

<br>
**Speaking**  
State the takeaway first.

::: notes
Few calls this salience. Writers call it a lead. Same idea.
:::

## Principle 2: Reduce Cognitive Load

<br>
**Writing**  
Short sentences. Everyday words.

<br>
**Visual**  
High contrast. No clutter.

<br>
**Speaking**  
Fewer points. Slower pace.

::: notes
This ties Visage + accessibility + Few together.
:::

## Principle 3: Make Differences Obvious

<br>
**Writing**  
Name the comparison explicitly.

<br>
**Visual**  
Direct labels > legends.

<br>
**Speaking**  
Higher / lower / changed after.

::: notes
Students often imply differences instead of stating them.
:::

## Principle 4: Context Creates Meaning

<br>
**Writing**  
Who is this for?

<br>
**Visual**  
Consistent scales and denominators.

<br>
**Speaking**  
Why should this audience care?

::: notes
Audience awareness without jargon.
:::

# Working With Examples

*Your first draft and final draft should be distant relatives.*


## 🎥 Example: A Clear Data Story  

<br><br>

- One way cognitive load was reduced 
- How is context provided to make the audience care 
- One visual choice that helps

<br><br>

:::: fragment
**Why this works:**
- Clear takeaway early  
- Simple visuals  
- Visuals support speech  
- Personal and Emotional but not Maudlin
::::

::: notes
Prompt students before video:  
One conclusion sentence  
One data-supported sentence  
One visual choice that helps
:::

## {data-background-image="media/images/w4_good_ex1.png" data-background-size="contain" data-background-position="center" data-background-repeat="no-repeat" }

:::: fragment
::: {.callout-tip title="How do we improve" }
<div style="background-color: #f8f8f8; padding: 0.25rem; border-radius: 4px;">
Why does this connect with the audience?
</div>
:::
::::

## {data-background-image="media/images/w4_bad_ex1.png" data-background-size="contain" data-background-position="center" data-background-repeat="no-repeat" }

:::: fragment
::: {.callout-tip title="After visibility drops crash severity goes up." }
<div style="background-color: #f8f8f8; padding: 0.25rem; border-radius: 4px;">
Which is easier to understand?
</div>
:::
::::

## {data-background-image="media/images/w4_bad_ex2.png" data-background-size="contain" data-background-position="center" data-background-repeat="no-repeat" }

:::: fragment
::: {.callout-tip title="How do we improve"}
<div style="background-color: #f8f8f8; padding: 0.25rem; border-radius: 4px;">
Rewrite the Title (Headline)
</div>
:::
::::


## {data-background-image="media/images/w4_like_subscribe.jpg" data-background-size="contain" data-background-position="center" data-background-repeat="no-repeat" data-background-color="black"}

## Activity 1: Headline Sprint (5 min)

<br>
1. Start with your paragraph.

<br>
2. Write **one sentence** stating the conclusion  

<br>
3. Cut it to **7 words or fewer**

<br>

::: {.callout-important title="We love words"}
Do not include any numbers.
:::

::: notes
Timer. Call on 2–3 students.
:::

## Activity 2: Data Sentence Sprint (5 min)

<br>
Use this template:

<br>

> After **[change/action]**,  
> **Y changed from A to B**,  
> especially for **group Z**.

<br>

Rewrite **one sentence** from your paragraph.

::: notes
This fixes rambling instantly.
:::

## {data-background-image="media/images/w4_bad_ex2.png" data-background-size="contain" data-background-position="center" data-background-repeat="no-repeat" }

::::: columns
::: {.column width="70%"}
:::: fragment
::: {.callout-tip title="Activity 3: Visual Message Check"}
<div style="background-color: #f8f8f8; padding: 0.25rem; border-radius: 4px;">
What is the one message of this chart?
</div>
:::
::::
:::
:::::


## {data-background-color="black"}

<div style="height: 60vh; display: flex; flex-direction: column; justify-content: center; align-items: center;">

<div style="font-size: 20rem; font-weight: 700; line-height: 1; color: #ffffff; margin: 2rem;">
3:30:3:30
</div>

<div style="display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(4, 1fr);
            gap: 12rem; width: 96%;
            margin-top: -10rem;
            color: #bdbdbd; font-size: 2rem; font-weight: 600;">

<div style="text-align:center;">seconds</div>
<div style="text-align:center;">seconds</div>
<div style="text-align:center;">minutes</div>
<div style="text-align:center;">minutes</div>

</div>
</div>


# Data doesn’t speak. You do.
*You know how to identify, summarize, visualize and communicate the stories in your data.*

::: notes
Confidence-building close.
:::
